outcome_text: Risks and benefits of all identified TEVV approaches are assessed and documented.
scoring_text: Ability to evaluate and compare TEVV approach trade-offs for AI systems, including assessing the risks and benefits of different testing, evaluation, verification, and validation methodologies, and documenting the rationale for selected TEVV approaches.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to evaluate and compare TEVV approach trade-offs for AI systems, including assessing the risks and benefits of different testing, evaluation, verification, and validation methodologies, and documenting the rationale for selected TEVV approaches.
reference_document_element_description: Design and execute AI system test and evaluation programs, producing documented test plans, results, identified failure modes, and deployment recommendations.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-3.4 (MAP) superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-T-003 is the strongest match (STS: 0.643, cosine: 0.615). Secondary: AI-T-007 (AI, cosine: 0.593), RM-K-006 (RM, cosine: 0.588).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.643). Shared vocabulary: [ai, evaluation]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [ability, approach, approaches, assessing, benefits, compare] vs WIDAI terms [deployment, design, documented, execute, failure, identified] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.882 (P=0.870, R=0.895). R exceeds P by 0.025 — the WIDAI KSA's 20 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 33 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (96.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.615 ≈ STS 0.643 (gap 0.027). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, evaluation] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [ai, evaluation]. NIST AI RMF-only: [ability, approach, approaches, assessing, benefits, compare]. WIDAI-only: [deployment, design, documented, execute, failure, identified].
